notes: VisualAge family discontinued April 30, 2007. Eclipse framework derived from VisualAge Micro Edition (donated by IBM 2001). VisualAge Smalltalk transferred to Instantiations as VA Smalltalk in 2005.
Upcoming at time of study (1997). VisualAge for Java evolved; Eclipse replaced it. VisualAge ME became Eclipse framework donated to open source 2001.
FlowMark graphical workflow modeling and execution. Evolved into IBM MQ Workflow; later IBM Business Process Manager (BPM).
Group development and repository management. Succeeded by IBM Rational ClearCase and other Rational products post-2003 acquisition.
Cross-platform application generation from platform-independent specifications. Evolved into IBM RDi (Rational Developer for i); still available for IBM i (AS/400 successor).
System Object Model / Distributed SOM. IBM's CORBA-based object sharing. Competed with Microsoft DCOM. Discontinued; DCOM won the desktop, CORBA/J2EE won enterprise middleware.
IBM/Apple compound document component architecture. Abandoned 1997 by Apple; IBM discontinued soon after. Lost to Microsoft OLE/ActiveX.
Conceptual modeling and physical data design tool for performance optimization. Superseded by IBM Data Studio and other tools.
Graphical business process mapping. Evolved into IBM WebSphere Business Modeler and eventually IBM Business Process Manager.
Middleware for scaling data-intensive Internet applications via comms multiplexing and load-balancing. Evolved into WebSphere Application Server transaction services.
